description: Nighttime view of illuminated city skyscrapers. The setting changes to the exterior of a coffee shop window reading "CENTRAL PERK."
Now inside the brightly lit coffee shop, several people sit on couches and chairs around a low table. A rhythmic instrumental tune plays, and the group begins humming along.
Ross sits on an orange couch, looking at a magazine and holding a mug, humming.
Chandler and Rachel sit at a nearby table. Rachel taps her pen on a notepad, humming along with the others.
Ross turns a page in his magazine. Monica and Phoebe sit together; Phoebe writes with an orange marker while both hum.
Ross raises a pencil in his right hand, making slight gestures while continuing to hum.
The group continues humming. Chandler reads a newspaper, and Joey sits beside him.
Phoebe and Monica look at each other, humming. Ross points his pencil forward.
Joey and Chandler look at their newspapers. Ross reaches his hand toward Monica, and she swats it away.
Ross points his pencil at Monica, who gestures back. Phoebe smiles, still humming and moving her marker.
Phoebe continues humming, "But up. But"
Joey turns toward Chandler. Ross looks down at his magazine, and the group stops humming.
Ross says, "do."
Chandler gestures with his hands and says, "No, we're done."
Chandler adds, "We're done."
Chandler lowers his hands. The screen turns black and remains silent.
The setting changes to a kitchen. Monica, holding a phone to her ear with her shoulder, places a pan of lasagna on top of several others on a table.

Monica stands by the lasagnas, speaking into the phone. She gestures with an oven mitt on her hand, "Stop yelling. All I'm saying is, if you had told me vegetarian lasagna, I would have made vegetarian lasagna."
Monica removes the oven mitt and holds the phone with her hand.
Monica places one hand on her hip and says into the phone, "Well, the meat only every third layer. Maybe you could scrape."
The setting changes to a living room where Joey, Chandler, Ross, and Phoebe sit around a coffee table.
Joey looks at an open book in his hands and asks, "Ross, did you really read all these baby books?"
Ross, sitting on the floor, points his finger and replies, "Yep. You could plunk me down in the middle of any woman's uterus, no compass,"
Ross gestures with his hand and snaps his fingers, adding, "and I could find my way out of there like that."
Joey lowers his gaze back to his book.
Joey says, "OOH, this is cool."
Reading from the book, Joey says, "It says in some parts of the world people actually eat the placenta." He then scrunches his face.
Chandler sits on the couch, holding a yogurt cup and a spoon.
Chandler puts down his spoon and places the cup on the table, saying, "And we're done with the yogurt."
Phoebe and Ross look toward Chandler. The setting changes back to the kitchen, where Monica stands holding the phone.
Monica gestures with her free hand and says, "Sorry, Aunt Syl. I did this as a favor."
Monica raises her voice, continuing, "I am not a caterer. What do you want me to do with a dozen lasagnas?"
Monica holds the phone to her ear, her facial expression remaining still.
Monica says, "Nice talk, Aunt Syl."
Monica pulls the phone slightly away from her ear.
Monica returns the phone to her ear and asks, "You kiss Uncle Freddie with that mouth?"
The setting changes back to the living room.
Joey looks at Ross, holding his thumb and index finger close together. "Hey, Ross, listen, do you know that right now your baby is only this big?"
Joey moves his pinched fingers slightly toward Ross and says, "This is your baby."
Joey speaks in a high, squeaky pitch, "Hi, Daddy." Ross smiles.
Joey keeps his fingers pinched and looks directly at them.
Joey, still using the high pitch, asks his fingers, "How come you don't live with Mommy?"
Ross's smile fades as he watches Joey.
Joey continues in the high pitch, "How come Mommy lives with that other lady?"
Ross stares at Joey. Joey lowers his hand and stops smiling.
Joey looks at Ross and raises his pinched fingers again.
Joey asks in the high pitch, "What's a lesbian?"
Ross continues to stare at Joey. The setting changes to an apartment door opening, revealing Paolo and Rachel holding hands.
Paolo and Rachel step inside. Paolo speaks while Rachel touches his face, "What? You probably topple the future. Poker nose. Poke a nose."
Rachel touches Paolo's nose and says, "It's like poke a nose."
Paolo touches Rachel's nose.
Paolo says, "Poor," and leans forward to kiss Rachel. They kiss while standing by the open doorway.
In the living room, Joey, Chandler, and Ross sit watching Rachel and Paolo kiss.
The setting changes to the kitchen. Monica stands near the table of lasagnas as Rachel enters, followed by Paolo.
Rachel pulls a bottle from the refrigerator and says, "Poconos. Yes."
Rachel turns toward Monica and Phoebe, holding the bottle, and adds, "My sister's giving us her place for the weekend."
Phoebe smiles at Rachel.
Phoebe says, "Woohoo. First weekend away together." Rachel smiles and replies, "Yeah, it's a big step."
The setting changes to the living room. Chandler sits on the couch, raising his hands.
Chandler says, "It's just a weekend. Big deal." Ross sits forward, moving his hands, and asks, "Wasn't this supposed to be just a huh?"
Ross continues, "Shouldn't it be"
Ross holds his hands out, pausing briefly.
Ross finishes his sentence, "flung by now?"

In a kitchen, Rachel stands wearing an apron, with Monica and Phoebe seated nearby. Rachel says, "I mean, we are way past the fling thing. I mean, I am feeling things that I've only read about in Danielle Steel books, you"
Rachel presses her hand to her chest. "know? I mean, when I'm with him, I'm just totally, totally..."
The setting changes to a living room, where Ross sits leaning forward in an armchair. He says, "...nauseous."
Ross continues, "I'm physically nauseous."
Ross sits with a mesh bag and papers on his lap. Chandler and Joey sit on the couch opposite him.
Ross opens his hands and asks, "What am I supposed to huh, call immigration?"
Ross pauses, staring straight ahead with a neutral expression.
Ross looks slightly to his left and adds, "I could call immigration."
Paolo walks into the living room from the background, waving his hands and speaking. Ross, Joey, and Chandler turn to look at him.
The setting shifts to the group—Monica, Rachel, Phoebe, Chandler, Joey, Ross, and Paolo—standing together and looking out a large window into the night.
Phoebe points toward the window and says, "Oh, look, ugly Naked Guy's making shadow puppets." The group continues watching.
The group continues looking out the window in silence.
A man says, "Oh, but look. It's Abraham Lincoln." The group remains looking out the window.
The setting shifts back to the living room, with Ross seated in his armchair and Joey and Chandler on the couch.
Ross waves his hand dismissively and says, "All right, go, go." Joey and Chandler quickly stand up and move toward the background.
Joey and Chandler join the rest of the group looking out the window.
The setting changes to a night view of an apartment building exterior, then shifts to a hallway inside. Joey walks holding a small item, followed by Chandler holding a large glass dish of lasagna.
Joey holds up his hands, curling his fingers as he smiles, and says in a high-pitched voice, "I love babies with their widdle baby shoes and their widdle baby toes."
Joey continues making a high-pitched vocalization, shaking his fists near his face while smiling.
Chandler points at Joey and says, "Okay, you're going to have to stop that forever." Joey lowers his hands and his smile fades.
Joey lowers his head, turns, and walks through the open door into an apartment. Chandler follows him.
Inside the apartment kitchen, Joey accidentally bumps into a round table, knocking it over. It falls to the floor with a loud crash.
Chandler and Joey stand still, looking down at the overturned table. They briefly exchange words.
The setting changes to a night view of the city skyline, then to the exterior of a brick apartment building.
The setting changes to an apartment interior. Carol opens the door to Ross, who stands outside.
Ross enters carrying a mesh bag and the lasagna dish. He and Carol greet each other with a kiss on the cheek.
Ross hands the dish to Carol and says, "And Monica sends her love along with this lasagna."
Carol smiles, taking the dish, and says, "Great. Is it vegetarian? Because Susan doesn't eat meat." She turns and walks toward the kitchen.
Ross watches Carol walk away, his expression neutral. He lowers the mesh bag.
Ross looks down slightly and says, "I'm pretty sure that it is."
Carol turns back to face Ross, smiling. Ross steps closer to her, extending his hands toward her stomach.
Ross lightly touches her stomach and says, "19 weeks." He then looks at her chest and adds, "The breasts are starting to swell."
Carol reacts with a look of surprise. Ross quickly raises his hands and says, "According to the literature."
Carol smiles broadly and says, "I got the results of the amnio today." Ross leans in and says, "Oh, tell me, tell me. Is everything..."
Carol finishes, "...totally and completely healthy." Ross smiles and responds, "That's great."
Ross embraces Carol and says, "That is great."
They release the hug. Ross looks down, picks up a framed photo from the counter, and examines it.
Ross holds the photo and asks, "Hey, when did you and Susan meet Huey Lewis?"
Carol smiles slightly and replies, "That's our friend Tanya."
Ross's smile drops as he stares at the photo. Carol watches him steadily.
Ross slowly lowers the photo frame, maintaining his gaze on it, before gently placing it back on the counter.
Ross nods slightly and says, "Of course it's your friend Tanya."
Carol places her hands on her hips and looks at Ross.
Carol asks, "Don't you all know about the sex?"
Ross looks at Carol in silence.
Ross touches his ear and repeats, "The sex?"
Ross looks away and gestures with his hands.
Ross gestures as he says, "Yeah, I'm having enough trouble with the image of you and Susan together"
Ross briefly lowers his hands.
Ross waves his hands near his head and adds, "when you throw in Tanya."
Carol drops her hands from her hips and sighs.
Carol points at her stomach and clarifies, "The sex of the baby, ross,"
Ross's eyes widen and his mouth opens in realization as he looks at her stomach.
Ross steps closer and points at her, asking, "you know the sex of the"
Carol smiles and nods, patting her stomach. Ross touches her arm and speaks briefly, to which she nods several times.
Ross quickly waves his hands in front of him and leans back. "no, I don't want to know. Absolutely not."
Ross gestures with both hands. "No, I think you shouldn't know until you look down there and see, oh, there it is."
Ross looks away briefly, then adds with a slight smile, "Or isn't."
Carol smiles as Ross looks back at her.

In an apartment living room, Susan, wearing a dark green turtleneck, enters through the front door, closing it behind her. Ross, wearing a brown jacket, and Carol, wearing a gray blazer, stand nearby.
Susan looks at Ross and says, "Hello, Ross." Ross replies, "Susan," keeping his gaze on her.
Carol steps toward Susan, opening her arms slightly as she moves closer.
Susan smiles and asks, "Did you hear?" Carol embraces Susan and says, "Yes, we did. Everything's a okay."
Carol and Susan hug, both smiling. Ross stands a few feet away, watching them. Audience laughter is audible.
Carol says, "It really is do." Still hugging, Susan says, "We certainly do." Carol adds, "It's going to."
Ross waves his hands rapidly in front of him, gesturing outward.
Ross points to himself with both thumbs and says, "It's the guy who doesn't want to know standing right here."
Susan lowers her arms and turns her gaze toward Carol, her eyes widening slightly.
Susan looks closely at Carol and asks, "Well, is it what we thought it would be?"
Carol smiles and nods her head.
Carol softly says, "Mmhmm."
Susan and Carol embrace again. Ross continues to stand near them, his gaze shifting. Audience laughter is audible.
Ross gestures toward them with one hand and says, "hey,"
Susan and Carol pull apart slightly, and Susan turns her head toward Ross.
Susan asks, "what?"
Ross points his finger at them and asks, "What did we think it was gonna be?"
Ross quickly shakes his head, waving both index fingers, and says, "No, I don't want to know. Don't want to know. Okay. You know, I should probably I should probably just go."
Carol nods and says, "Well, thanks for the book." Ross replies, "No problem. Okay," while stepping toward her.
Ross leans in and kisses Carol on the cheek, then steps back and turns his attention toward Susan.
Ross gives a small wave and says, "Susan."
Susan raises her hand flat near her shoulder in a brief wave as Ross turns and walks past her toward the front door.
Ross exits the apartment. Carol closes the door behind him while Susan walks to the sofa and sits down, leaning back.
Sitting on the sofa, Susan asks, "All right, who should we call first? Your folks or Devin Rona?"
Carol walks over to a wall-mounted intercom panel near the door and leans toward it, raising one hand to press a button.
Carol speaks into the intercom, asking, "Hello?"
Ross's voice is heard stuttering over the intercom speaker while Carol listens, resting her hand on the panel.
Ross's voice says over the intercom, "Never mind. I don't want to know."
Carol smiles and turns her head away from the intercom panel.

The setting changes to Chandler and Joey's apartment kitchen. Chandler, wearing a patterned short-sleeve shirt, and Joey, in a brown t-shirt, sit facing each other at a small table. A broken glass-topped table lies collapsed on the floor nearby. Chandler eats from a casserole dish and says, "Okay, but just because it was my table, I have to buy a new one. That's the rule."
Joey looks at Chandler, holding his fork in one hand.
Joey gestures widely with his free hand and says, "What rule? There's no rule. If anything, you owe me a table."
Chandler points with his fork, asking, "How'd you get there?" Joey leans forward and replies, "Well, I believe this piece of furniture was fine until your little breakfast adventure with Angela del"
Joey finishes, "Vecchio." Chandler's eyes widen as he asks, "You knew about that?" Joey gestures and says, "Well, let's just say the impressions you made in the butter left little to the"
Joey continues gesturing with his hand. Chandler stops eating and stares at him. Audience laughter is audible.
Chandler points both index fingers at Joey and says, "okay. Okay. How about if we split it?" Joey asks, "What do you mean, like buy it together?" Chandler replies, "Yeah."
Joey lowers his gaze toward his food and asks in a lower volume, "You think we're ready for something like that?"
Chandler continues eating, looking thoughtfully at Joey as audience laughter sounds.
Chandler asks, "Why not?"
Joey shrugs slightly and says, "Well, just that it's a pretty big commitment."

Joey gestures with his fork, asking, "I mean, what if one of us wants to move out?" Chandler replies, speaking faster than his previous line, "Why are you moving out? I'm not moving out. You tell me if you"

Joey points to his chest, saying, "were moving, right?" Chandler nods, replying, "Yes, yes." Joey points a finger at Chandler, stating, "It's just that with my last roommate, Kip," and Chandler interrupts, "I know all about"
Chandler picks up his casserole dish and stands up from his chair.

Chandler walks over to the kitchen counter, saying, "that. We bought a hibachi together, and then he ran off and got married and things got pretty ugly." Joey turns in his seat, points to himself, and says, "Let me ask you something."
Joey looks at Chandler and asks, "Was Kip a better roommate than me?"
Chandler raises his hand with his palm facing Joey and says, "Oh, don't do"
Chandler lowers his hand and says, "that's."
The view changes to the New York City skyline under a partly cloudy sky. Upbeat instrumental music plays.

The setting changes to a brightly lit massage room. Phoebe, wearing a brown vest over a long-sleeve shirt, smooths a blue blanket over a massage table as a woman holding a clipboard walks toward her and says, "You've got a"
The woman gestures with her pen and finishes, "couple changes in your schedule."
The woman raises one hand, counting on her fingers, and says, "Your 04:00 herbal massage has been pushed back to 430. And Miss Summerfield canceled her 530."
The woman says, "Shiatsu." Phoebe nods and replies, "Okay, thanks." The woman gestures toward the door, turns around, and says, "Oh, here comes your 03:00."
The woman turns back to Phoebe with a smile and says, "I don't mean to sound unprofessional, but yum."
The woman walks past Paolo, who enters the room wearing a white bathrobe.
Paolo smiles at Phoebe and says, "Buongiorno, bella Phoebe."
Phoebe's eyes widen, and she smiles broadly, saying, "Oh, Paolo."
Phoebe laughs briefly and asks, "Hi. What are you doing here?"
Paolo gestures toward himself with both hands, stepping closer to the massage table.
Paolo gestures expansively and says, "Raquel tell me you massage." Phoebe nods, adjusting her posture, and replies, "Yeah, well, Raquel is right."
Phoebe nods emphatically and says, "Yeah."
Paolo smiles at Phoebe.
Phoebe smiles back and shifts her weight slightly as they stand near the massage table.
Inside a room with a massage table, Paolo stands wearing a white bathrobe. Phoebe stands nearby holding a blue sheet.
Phoebe says, "Okay. I don't know what you just said, so let's start."
Paolo gestures to his chest.
Paolo says, "I am"
Paolo hesitates slightly, his hands moving near his robe's tie.

Paolo continues, "being naked." Phoebe replies, "That's really your decision? I mean, some people prefer to take up" as Paolo opens his robe, exposing his bare chest and legs. Phoebe steps back quickly.
The setting changes to a coffee shop. Rachel, wearing a waitress apron, holds a coffee pot near the central seating area where Ross sits on a couch.
Rachel gestures toward Ross and says, "I can't believe you don't want to know. I mean, I couldn't not know. I mean, if the doctor knows and Carol knows and Susan knows"
Monica, seated at a nearby small table, adds, "and Monica knows"
Ross turns his head toward Monica, his eyes widening.
Ross points at Monica and asks, "how could you know? I don't even know."
Monica replies, "Carol called me to thank me for the lasagna. I asked." Joey, sitting next to Chandler, says, "You told me." Joey asks Ross, "So, what's it going to?"
Ross stands up quickly, his movements abrupt.
Ross gestures broadly and shouts, "Huh? Oh, great. Now he knows and I don't know." Monica responds, "I'm sorry. I'm just excited about being an aunt."
Joey points to himself and says, "Or an uncle."
Ross stares at Joey, then turns and walks away.
Phoebe enters the coffee shop wearing a large patterned coat and walks past the group.
Rachel says, "Hey, Phoebe." Another voice adds, "Hey, phoebe". Phoebe replies, "fine." without stopping.
Phoebe sits down heavily on the couch.
Monica leans toward her and asks, "Phoebe, what's the matter?" Phoebe replies, "Nothing."
Phoebe says, "I'm sorry. I'm just I'm out of sorts." Chandler says, "Well, you can use some of my sorts."
Chandler drinks from his mug and adds, "I rarely use them."
An older woman seated at another table calls out, "Hey, can we get some cappuccino over here?"
Rachel stands still, looking toward the seating area.
Rachel startles, saying, "Oh, right. That's me," and hurries away.
Joey looks at his watch and nudges Chandler.
Joey stands and says, "Hey, Chandler, that table place closed at seven. Come on." Chandler stands, replying, "Fine."
Monica moves to sit on the couch next to Phoebe.
Monica asks, "What is it?"
Phoebe turns her head toward Monica.
Phoebe says, "All right. You know Paolo?" Ross, seated in an armchair, replies, "I'm familiar with his work, yes."
Phoebe lowers her gaze.
Phoebe says, "He made a move on me." Joey, standing near the door, reacts with, "Whoa."
Joey puts on his jacket and says, "Store will be open tomorrow."
Chandler gestures toward the couch and says, "Coffee over here, please." They rush back toward the seating area.
Chandler and Joey kneel near the coffee table, looking closely at Phoebe.

Monica asks, "Well, what happened?" The setting changes to the massage room, where Paolo lies face down on the table, covered to his waist with a blue sheet. Phoebe massages his back. Her voiceover begins, "Well, he came in for a massage and everything was fine until"
Paolo's hand reaches up and grips Phoebe's thigh. She stops massaging and stares at his hand. The setting returns to the coffee shop, where the group gasps loudly.
Monica asks, "my God, are you sure?"
In a brief return to the massage room, Phoebe massages Paolo's bare buttocks.
Back in the coffee shop, Phoebe smiles briefly and says, "Oh, yeah, I'm sure."
Chandler and Joey stare silently at Phoebe. Monica turns her attention back to Phoebe. The setting changes back to the massage room, where Phoebe continues massaging Paolo's back.
Phoebe's voiceover continues, "And all of a sudden, his hands weren't the problem anymore," as Paolo rolls onto his back. Phoebe steps back quickly.
Paolo lies on his back with the sheet over his lower half. The setting returns to the coffee shop, where Phoebe sits with her hands resting on her legs.
Monica asks, "was it?" Phoebe gestures with her hands and says, "Oh, Boy Scouts could have camped under there."
Chandler and Joey continue to stare at Phoebe. Monica looks at Phoebe.
Monica asks, "Wow. So what'd you do?" Phoebe replies, "Well, I dealt with it like a professional". The setting changes to the massage room.
Phoebe throws the blue sheet over Paolo's head. The setting returns to the coffee shop, where Phoebe finishes her sentence, mimicking a covering motion.
Rachel approaches the group from behind the couch, raising her hands as Phoebe continues speaking inaudibly.
Rachel says, "Thurman."
Several voices chime in, and Chandler says, "Oh, the actress."
Rachel high-fives Chandler and Joey.
Chandler says, "Thanks, Rach."
Chandler stands up and steps closer to the group.
Chandler says, "So what are you going to do?" Ross points toward Phoebe and says, "You have to tell her. You have to."
The screen turns black.
The screen remains black.
